item.title: The 12 Greatest Single-Player Open-World Games Of All Time, Ranked
item.description:
The idea of an open-world has been ever-present in gaming since its earliest days. While hardware limitations may have kept the scope and fidelity of these open spaces from reaching the highs we see today, the adventure titles of yesteryear laid the groundwork for what would come later. The ability to travel vast environments, tackle quests in a semi-malleable order, and explore unknown ruins at a player’s leisure goes back all the way to the 1970s and 80s with titles like Ultima I .

However, advancements in technology have paved the way for some truly immersive experiences and impressive spectacles. Whether the game is aiming for a more realistic approach to a world, a la Red Dead Redemption 2 , creating a sandbox of wonder like Minecraft , or telling an intricate story, open-world games deliver some of the very best times the space has to offer, and these eleven titles stand at the very pinnacle.

12 Red Dead Redemption 2

One Last Job, One Last Ride

Red Dead Redemption 2
10 /10
Released
October 26, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC, Stadia
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

Considered by many to be Rockstar’s greatest achievement, Red Dead Redemption 2 is a stunning work. Taking place during the decline of America’s Wild West in 1899, Red Dead Redemption 2 follows Arthur Morgan, a member of the notorious Van der Linde outlaw gang, which now finds itself on the run.

Following a botched robbery in Blackwater, the gang is forced to flee while being pursued by federal agents and relentless bounty hunters. As internal fractures and moral dilemmas threaten to tear the gang apart, Arthur must ultimately choose between his personal ideals and his unwavering loyalty to the surrogate family that he’s been a member of for so long, all while dealing with health issues of his own.

The freedom to tackle side missions whenever players desire, or engage in various extracurricular activities, is staggering; as is the detail with which everything is portrayed. Every NPC has a life to live, with set schedules, jobs, and families they attend to – they even react to Arthur’s appearance and behavior, as well as behave differently due to the weather.

11 Fallout: New Vegas

The House Always Wins

Fallout: New Vegas
8 /10
Released
October 19, 2010

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

While Bethesda's handling of the iconic franchise has been met with criticism, Obsidian's entry into the series has long stood as one of the very best open-world games out there. Set in the post-apocalyptic Mojave Desert, Fallout: New Vegas is an exercise in role-playing driven by player freedom. Players take on the role of the Courier, a delivery person who has been shot in the head and left for dead over a mysterious Platinum Chip. After surviving, the Courier’s quest for vengeance quickly spirals into a massive war between factions vying for power in the Mojave.

Players must navigate the bitter conflict between the expansionist New California Republic, the totalitarian Caesar’s Legion, and the enigmatic Mr. House, with player choices ultimately deciding who will control the Hoover Dam and shape the future of the Mojave wasteland.

10 Subnautica

Might Be Paradise If It Weren’t For The Deadly Fish

Subnautica
Released
January 23, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
GGMods Mods

Platform(s)
Nintendo Switch, PC, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
Genre(s)
Survival, Horror
Powered by

Subnautica is a phenomenal open-world survival adventure game set on a breathtaking alien planet covered almost entirely by water. After crash-landing, players must explore a vibrant, treacherous ocean to survive. The experience neatly contrasts serene underwater beauty with intense psychological horror, as players must manage vital oxygen levels, scavenge raw resources, construct high-tech habitats, and craft advanced submersibles while swimming through some of the most breathtaking underwater views ever put into a game.

As players delve into the depths of the alien ocean, they’ll explore coral reefs and pitch-black abysses alike, as well as encounter the terrifying Leviathan predators. Driven by a compelling mystery, players must uncover ancient alien secrets to cure a deadly virus and escape from this hostile, yet mesmerizing world.

9 No Man’s Sky

An Infinite Sky to Explore

No Man’s Sky is an infinite, procedurally generated sci-fi exploration game that invites players to adventure through an uncharted universe. Players can seamlessly transition from deep space to alien surfaces, where they’ll find peculiar flora and fauna to research ot harvest.

While it originally launched with high expectations, the game was a bit of a disappointment for many. However, through many free updates, it has been transformed into an impressive example of post-release redemption. Whether players prefer base-building, spaceship dogfights, or exploring alien ruins, the cosmos is entirely theirs to explore and shape.

8 Elden Ring

Rise, Tarnished, and Become the Elden Lord

Elden Ring
10 /10
Released
February 25, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Powered by

Elden Ring features a sprawling, ruined expanse called the Lands Between, which houses both formidable foes and deadly environments. Players must traverse a vast, seamless open world and battle corrupted demigods to claim the title of Elden Lord for themselves, looting treasure and equipment along the way.

Renowned for its difficult combat, cryptic storytelling, and freedom of exploration, Elden Ring is one of the most well-rounded and exciting open-world games on the market—even if the exact history and story of the game can be a bit nebulous. It showcases an unforgettable atmosphere and makes players earn every new tidbit of information received through item descriptions and environmental storytelling, turning every new find into a mystery to unfold.

7 Cyberpunk 2077

Take In That Night City Air

Cyberpunk 2077
6 /10
Released
December 10, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Powered by

Cyberpunk 2077 is set in the megalopolis of Night City, a neon-lit corporate dystopia where gangs and corporations rule the town in equal measure. Players control V, a mercenary navigating a city obsessed with power, glamour, and cybernetic body modification. Night City is among gaming's most impressive and immersive cities, with each element feeling coherent to the established history of this world.

After a major heist goes wrong, V becomes fused with The Relic, a prototype biochip containing the digital consciousness of legendary rockerboy Johnny Silverhand. As this chip threatens to permanently overwrite V’s mind, players must uncover corporate conspiracies, battle dangerous gangs, and run through the net. It delivers an immersive experience unlike any other, where critical player choices shape the direction of the game's endings.

6 The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
10 /10
Released
March 3, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Nintendo EPD
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
Nintendo Wii U, Switch
Powered by

Nintendo wasn't particularly known for open-world games before 2017, but that changed when The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild came out and rejuvenated the genre. Except for a short cutscene at the start, the game almost immediately drops Link (and players) into a massive version of Hyrule and tells them to go explore. The only "must-do" mission is to defeat Calamity Ganon, something that can be tackled immediately, after 15 hours, or after 100 hours. Players decide what they do, when they do it, and how much of it they do. Like most Zelda games, BOTW is light on plot but rich in story, with the world being filled with NPCs and quests that develop organically.

If you are the type of player who prefers to have goals set for them, BOTW 's open-ended nature might feel a bit too overwhelming, but you are still given four general quests to complete that help provide some structure if it is necessary. Although the melee combat is very simple, it is elevated by the Sheihak Runes, which are available immediately, and the Champion abilities, which are unlocked by completing the four main quests, which can be used in a variety of ways that turn battles into emergent puzzles.

5 Minecraft

Build Your Dreams, Slay The Dragon

Minecraft
Released
November 18, 2011

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Sandbox, Survival
Platform(s)
3DS, Android, iOS, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Wii U, PC, PlayStation 3, PlayStation 4, PS Vita, Xbox One, Xbox 360
Powered by

Minecraft was a cultural phenomenon from the moment it was released, and has arrested the attention of multiple generations of gamers thanks to its open gameplay and procedurally generated worlds. No two seeds are alike, with each new world feeling unique in its own right, with varied topography and resources available for players to tinker with.

Subscribe to the newsletter for open-world game picks

Want more exploration, choices, and worlds to lose yourself in? Subscribe to the newsletter for curated open-world game recommendations, deep-dive analyses, hidden-gem discoveries, and thoughtful write-ups that help you find what to play next, plus other
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

In Survival Mode, players must manage health and hunger while fending off monsters like zombies and Creepers, and can even make their way through a netherworld or enter the final fight against a massive dragon. Alternatively, Creative Mode offers unlimited resources and the ability to fly, giving players the tools to showcase their creativity. With its iconic pixelated aesthetic and open-ended gameplay, Minecraft is a phenomenal sandbox where the only limit is a player’s imagination.

4 Outer Wilds

22 Minutes to Save the Universe... What Could Go Wrong?

Outer Wilds
Released
May 28, 2019

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Adventure
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC
Powered by

In Outer Wilds , players control an alien astronaut trapped in a 22-minute time loop. Exploring a miniature solar system, players must uncover the mysteries of an extinct ancient civilization before the sun goes supernova, resetting the cycle. Progression is entirely knowledge-based; there are no traditional upgrades or unlocked items. Instead, players use the information gathered across countless loops to navigate environmental puzzles and ultimately discover the secrets of the universe.

Outer Wilds is one of those games where players should go in as blind as possible—the exploration is really the point. Uncovering the hidden information, seeing the varied planets, and meeting new characters is a wonder to behold on the first run through the game. If you're stuck, some pointers might be helpful, provided they avoid spoilers.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

3 Yakuza 0

Turf Wars on the Lot and on the Dance Floor

Yakuza 0
Released
January 24, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Beat 'Em Up
Powered by

Set amongst the vibrant excess of 1988 Japan, Yakuza 0 is a prequel to the acclaimed Yakuza series, showing us the origins of some of the most memorable characters in the franchise. The story follows two protagonists: Kazuma Kiryu, a young yakuza framed for murder, and Goro Majima, an exiled gangster managing a cabaret club to earn his way back into the good graces of his patriarch. Both men become entangled in a violent gang war over the Empty Lot, a tiny but incredibly valuable piece of real estate crucial to an enormous redevelopment project.

The towns of Kamurocho and Sotenbori serve as playgrounds where players can sit down for a round of Majong, sing karaoke, and even race RC cars. There is no shortage of games to play or things to see, and a bevy of side content to enjoy.

For all of its hilarious, or even downright bizarre, moments, Yakuza 0 tells a surprisingly heartwrenching and dramatic tale, weaving threads of redemption, identity, and economic anxiety in between bombastic battles and mostly-naked, dancing old guys. Yakuza makes no compromises in delivering one of the most engaging and unique gaming experiences to date.
item.content:encoded:
The idea of an open-world has been ever-present in gaming since its earliest days. While hardware limitations may have kept the scope and fidelity of these open spaces from reaching the highs we see today, the adventure titles of yesteryear laid the groundwork for what would come later. The ability to travel vast environments, tackle quests in a semi-malleable order, and explore unknown ruins at a player’s leisure goes back all the way to the 1970s and 80s with titles like Ultima I .

However, advancements in technology have paved the way for some truly immersive experiences and impressive spectacles. Whether the game is aiming for a more realistic approach to a world, a la Red Dead Redemption 2 , creating a sandbox of wonder like Minecraft , or telling an intricate story, open-world games deliver some of the very best times the space has to offer, and these eleven titles stand at the very pinnacle.

12 Red Dead Redemption 2

One Last Job, One Last Ride

Red Dead Redemption 2
10 /10
Released
October 26, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki

Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC, Stadia
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Powered by

Considered by many to be Rockstar’s greatest achievement, Red Dead Redemption 2 is a stunning work. Taking place during the decline of America’s Wild West in 1899, Red Dead Redemption 2 follows Arthur Morgan, a member of the notorious Van der Linde outlaw gang, which now finds itself on the run.

Following a botched robbery in Blackwater, the gang is forced to flee while being pursued by federal agents and relentless bounty hunters. As internal fractures and moral dilemmas threaten to tear the gang apart, Arthur must ultimately choose between his personal ideals and his unwavering loyalty to the surrogate family that he’s been a member of for so long, all while dealing with health issues of his own.

The freedom to tackle side missions whenever players desire, or engage in various extracurricular activities, is staggering; as is the detail with which everything is portrayed. Every NPC has a life to live, with set schedules, jobs, and families they attend to – they even react to Arthur’s appearance and behavior, as well as behave differently due to the weather.

11 Fallout: New Vegas

The House Always Wins

Fallout: New Vegas
8 /10
Released
October 19, 2010

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

While Bethesda's handling of the iconic franchise has been met with criticism, Obsidian's entry into the series has long stood as one of the very best open-world games out there. Set in the post-apocalyptic Mojave Desert, Fallout: New Vegas is an exercise in role-playing driven by player freedom. Players take on the role of the Courier, a delivery person who has been shot in the head and left for dead over a mysterious Platinum Chip. After surviving, the Courier’s quest for vengeance quickly spirals into a massive war between factions vying for power in the Mojave.

Players must navigate the bitter conflict between the expansionist New California Republic, the totalitarian Caesar’s Legion, and the enigmatic Mr. House, with player choices ultimately deciding who will control the Hoover Dam and shape the future of the Mojave wasteland.

10 Subnautica

Might Be Paradise If It Weren’t For The Deadly Fish

Subnautica
Released
January 23, 2018

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
GGMods Mods

Platform(s)
Nintendo Switch, PC, PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox One, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
Genre(s)
Survival, Horror
Powered by

Subnautica is a phenomenal open-world survival adventure game set on a breathtaking alien planet covered almost entirely by water. After crash-landing, players must explore a vibrant, treacherous ocean to survive. The experience neatly contrasts serene underwater beauty with intense psychological horror, as players must manage vital oxygen levels, scavenge raw resources, construct high-tech habitats, and craft advanced submersibles while swimming through some of the most breathtaking underwater views ever put into a game.

As players delve into the depths of the alien ocean, they’ll explore coral reefs and pitch-black abysses alike, as well as encounter the terrifying Leviathan predators. Driven by a compelling mystery, players must uncover ancient alien secrets to cure a deadly virus and escape from this hostile, yet mesmerizing world.

9 No Man’s Sky

An Infinite Sky to Explore

No Man’s Sky is an infinite, procedurally generated sci-fi exploration game that invites players to adventure through an uncharted universe. Players can seamlessly transition from deep space to alien surfaces, where they’ll find peculiar flora and fauna to research ot harvest.

While it originally launched with high expectations, the game was a bit of a disappointment for many. However, through many free updates, it has been transformed into an impressive example of post-release redemption. Whether players prefer base-building, spaceship dogfights, or exploring alien ruins, the cosmos is entirely theirs to explore and shape.

8 Elden Ring

Rise, Tarnished, and Become the Elden Lord

Elden Ring
10 /10
Released
February 25, 2022

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Powered by

Elden Ring features a sprawling, ruined expanse called the Lands Between, which houses both formidable foes and deadly environments. Players must traverse a vast, seamless open world and battle corrupted demigods to claim the title of Elden Lord for themselves, looting treasure and equipment along the way.

Renowned for its difficult combat, cryptic storytelling, and freedom of exploration, Elden Ring is one of the most well-rounded and exciting open-world games on the market—even if the exact history and story of the game can be a bit nebulous. It showcases an unforgettable atmosphere and makes players earn every new tidbit of information received through item descriptions and environmental storytelling, turning every new find into a mystery to unfold.

7 Cyberpunk 2077

Take In That Night City Air

Cyberpunk 2077
6 /10
Released
December 10, 2020

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL
FX Wiki Wiki GGMods Mods

Genre(s)
RPG, Action
Powered by

Cyberpunk 2077 is set in the megalopolis of Night City, a neon-lit corporate dystopia where gangs and corporations rule the town in equal measure. Players control V, a mercenary navigating a city obsessed with power, glamour, and cybernetic body modification. Night City is among gaming's most impressive and immersive cities, with each element feeling coherent to the established history of this world.

After a major heist goes wrong, V becomes fused with The Relic, a prototype biochip containing the digital consciousness of legendary rockerboy Johnny Silverhand. As this chip threatens to permanently overwrite V’s mind, players must uncover corporate conspiracies, battle dangerous gangs, and run through the net. It delivers an immersive experience unlike any other, where critical player choices shape the direction of the game's endings.

6 The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild

The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
10 /10
Released
March 3, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Developer(s)
Nintendo EPD
Genre(s)
Action, Adventure
Platform(s)
Nintendo Wii U, Switch
Powered by

Nintendo wasn't particularly known for open-world games before 2017, but that changed when The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild came out and rejuvenated the genre. Except for a short cutscene at the start, the game almost immediately drops Link (and players) into a massive version of Hyrule and tells them to go explore. The only "must-do" mission is to defeat Calamity Ganon, something that can be tackled immediately, after 15 hours, or after 100 hours. Players decide what they do, when they do it, and how much of it they do. Like most Zelda games, BOTW is light on plot but rich in story, with the world being filled with NPCs and quests that develop organically.

If you are the type of player who prefers to have goals set for them, BOTW 's open-ended nature might feel a bit too overwhelming, but you are still given four general quests to complete that help provide some structure if it is necessary. Although the melee combat is very simple, it is elevated by the Sheihak Runes, which are available immediately, and the Champion abilities, which are unlocked by completing the four main quests, which can be used in a variety of ways that turn battles into emergent puzzles.

5 Minecraft

Build Your Dreams, Slay The Dragon

Minecraft
Released
November 18, 2011

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Sandbox, Survival
Platform(s)
3DS, Android, iOS, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Wii U, PC, PlayStation 3, PlayStation 4, PS Vita, Xbox One, Xbox 360
Powered by

Minecraft was a cultural phenomenon from the moment it was released, and has arrested the attention of multiple generations of gamers thanks to its open gameplay and procedurally generated worlds. No two seeds are alike, with each new world feeling unique in its own right, with varied topography and resources available for players to tinker with.

Subscribe to the newsletter for open-world game picks

Want more exploration, choices, and worlds to lose yourself in? Subscribe to the newsletter for curated open-world game recommendations, deep-dive analyses, hidden-gem discoveries, and thoughtful write-ups that help you find what to play next, plus other
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

In Survival Mode, players must manage health and hunger while fending off monsters like zombies and Creepers, and can even make their way through a netherworld or enter the final fight against a massive dragon. Alternatively, Creative Mode offers unlimited resources and the ability to fly, giving players the tools to showcase their creativity. With its iconic pixelated aesthetic and open-ended gameplay, Minecraft is a phenomenal sandbox where the only limit is a player’s imagination.

4 Outer Wilds

22 Minutes to Save the Universe... What Could Go Wrong?

Outer Wilds
Released
May 28, 2019

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Adventure
Platform(s)
PS4, Xbox One, PC
Powered by

In Outer Wilds , players control an alien astronaut trapped in a 22-minute time loop. Exploring a miniature solar system, players must uncover the mysteries of an extinct ancient civilization before the sun goes supernova, resetting the cycle. Progression is entirely knowledge-based; there are no traditional upgrades or unlocked items. Instead, players use the information gathered across countless loops to navigate environmental puzzles and ultimately discover the secrets of the universe.

Outer Wilds is one of those games where players should go in as blind as possible—the exploration is really the point. Uncovering the hidden information, seeing the varied planets, and meeting new characters is a wonder to behold on the first run through the game. If you're stuck, some pointers might be helpful, provided they avoid spoilers.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

3 Yakuza 0

Turf Wars on the Lot and on the Dance Floor

Yakuza 0
Released
January 24, 2017

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Action, Adventure, Beat 'Em Up
Powered by

Set amongst the vibrant excess of 1988 Japan, Yakuza 0 is a prequel to the acclaimed Yakuza series, showing us the origins of some of the most memorable characters in the franchise. The story follows two protagonists: Kazuma Kiryu, a young yakuza framed for murder, and Goro Majima, an exiled gangster managing a cabaret club to earn his way back into the good graces of his patriarch. Both men become entangled in a violent gang war over the Empty Lot, a tiny but incredibly valuable piece of real estate crucial to an enormous redevelopment project.

The towns of Kamurocho and Sotenbori serve as playgrounds where players can sit down for a round of Majong, sing karaoke, and even race RC cars. There is no shortage of games to play or things to see, and a bevy of side content to enjoy.

For all of its hilarious, or even downright bizarre, moments, Yakuza 0 tells a surprisingly heartwrenching and dramatic tale, weaving threads of redemption, identity, and economic anxiety in between bombastic battles and mostly-naked, dancing old guys. Yakuza makes no compromises in delivering one of the most engaging and unique gaming experiences to date.

item.title: Early Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic Remake Cinematic Leaks Online
item.description:
An early cinematic for the canceled Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake has apparently leaked online, offering hints at what Aspyr was planning to do with the game. Released in 2003 on the original Xbox, Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic is widely considered one of the greatest video games of all-time. The game quickly earned a huge fan following, with a direct sequel released in 2004.

A Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake was announced for PS5 in 2021, with Aspyr handling the game's development duties. A year later, it was revealed that Aspyr's version of the remake was scrapped, with development duties shifting to Saber Interactive, instead. The current state of the game is a mystery, with many Star Wars fans speculating that it might never actually be released.

A New Look at Aspyr's KOTOR Remake

Over the years, elements from Aspyr's Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake have leaked, including concept art and development images. Now it seems an early cinematic for the game has also made its way online. According to MP1st, the cinematic was discovered through the portfolio of an artist that previously worked at Aspyr. The video in question still needed a lot of work, as the characters and voices are placeholders, and the environments are unfinished. However, fans of KOTOR can still glean some interesting things from the video.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0

For one thing, it seems the remake would have opened in a fashion similar to the original game. In the cinematic, the player character is being escorted by Trask Ulgo, as the Ender Spire finds itself under attack. The two plan to meet up with Carth Onasi, but must reestablish communications first. In an interesting change from the original game, it seems that Aspyr was planning to have the player character fully voiced, in addition to the rest of the cast. However, it's impossible to know if that would have been the case for the finished product, given that the voice work is clearly meant as a placeholder.

In March, Saber Interactive confirmed that its version of the Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake is still in development. Given the messy development cycle, fans can hardly be blamed for being skeptical. Nearly five years have passed since the initial announcement, and four years since Saber started things over from scratch. It's possible Saber and parent company Embracer Group are waiting until the game is far into development before showing the game off, to avoid negative publicity for the project. However, there's no way of knowing for certain.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

No one quite knows what to make of the KOTOR remake, but that doesn't mean it's all bad news; a spiritual successor to the series was announced last year. Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic will reunite several KOTOR veterans, including director Casey Hudson. A release date for the project has not been announced, but Hudson has made it clear that he wants the game available before 2030. For multiple reasons, that might be ambitious, and it will be interesting to see which of these games will be released first.

Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 83 /100 Critics Rec: 85%
Released
July 15, 2003
ESRB
T for Teen: Violence
Developer(s)
BioWare
Publisher(s)
LucasArts

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
An early cinematic for the canceled Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake has apparently leaked online, offering hints at what Aspyr was planning to do with the game. Released in 2003 on the original Xbox, Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic is widely considered one of the greatest video games of all-time. The game quickly earned a huge fan following, with a direct sequel released in 2004.

A Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake was announced for PS5 in 2021, with Aspyr handling the game's development duties. A year later, it was revealed that Aspyr's version of the remake was scrapped, with development duties shifting to Saber Interactive, instead. The current state of the game is a mystery, with many Star Wars fans speculating that it might never actually be released.

A New Look at Aspyr's KOTOR Remake

Over the years, elements from Aspyr's Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake have leaked, including concept art and development images. Now it seems an early cinematic for the game has also made its way online. According to MP1st, the cinematic was discovered through the portfolio of an artist that previously worked at Aspyr. The video in question still needed a lot of work, as the characters and voices are placeholders, and the environments are unfinished. However, fans of KOTOR can still glean some interesting things from the video.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0

For one thing, it seems the remake would have opened in a fashion similar to the original game. In the cinematic, the player character is being escorted by Trask Ulgo, as the Ender Spire finds itself under attack. The two plan to meet up with Carth Onasi, but must reestablish communications first. In an interesting change from the original game, it seems that Aspyr was planning to have the player character fully voiced, in addition to the rest of the cast. However, it's impossible to know if that would have been the case for the finished product, given that the voice work is clearly meant as a placeholder.

In March, Saber Interactive confirmed that its version of the Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake is still in development. Given the messy development cycle, fans can hardly be blamed for being skeptical. Nearly five years have passed since the initial announcement, and four years since Saber started things over from scratch. It's possible Saber and parent company Embracer Group are waiting until the game is far into development before showing the game off, to avoid negative publicity for the project. However, there's no way of knowing for certain.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

No one quite knows what to make of the KOTOR remake, but that doesn't mean it's all bad news; a spiritual successor to the series was announced last year. Star Wars: Fate of the Old Republic will reunite several KOTOR veterans, including director Casey Hudson. A release date for the project has not been announced, but Hudson has made it clear that he wants the game available before 2030. For multiple reasons, that might be ambitious, and it will be interesting to see which of these games will be released first.

Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic

Follow
Followed
RPG
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 83 /100 Critics Rec: 85%
Released
July 15, 2003
ESRB
T for Teen: Violence
Developer(s)
BioWare
Publisher(s)
LucasArts

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
RPG
Powered by

item.title: Black Ops 7 Zombies: All Maps Tier List
item.description:
Black Ops 7 Zombies currently features 4 Standard maps and 5 Survival maps, and you might be wondering where each of these maps ranks on a tier list. Below, we've ranked all 4 Standard maps against each other, and all 5 Survival maps to see which is the most fun, replayable, and exciting experience.

For Standard maps, we've factored in wonder weapons, easter eggs, general layout, and most importantly: fun. Take a look at the tier list below and check to see where your favorite map landed on our tier list and compare it to your own!

All Standard Maps Tier List

Map

Why?

S-Tier

Totenreich

The general vibe of Totenreich is immaculate, and while the easter egg quest is a tad shorter than most, the boss fight, map layout, and wonder weapon are heaps of fun. Besides, who doesn't like more Richtofen lore?

Ashes of the Damned

The launch map has so much to offer and proves that Treyarch can still innovate. Driving around Ol' Tessie across multiple locations, each with their own aesthetics, is a treat that makes this large map feel so very alive.

A-Tier

None

We don't think there are any A-Tier maps in BO7 Zombies yet, but the next two planned maps could change that.

B-Tier

Astra Malorum

There's no skybox quite like Astra Malorum, and there's certainly a lot to enjoy in terms of side easter eggs. However, the closed-in areas make this map a tad challenging, and O.S.C.A.R. can be even more annoying than George Romero from COTD.

C-Tier

Paradox Junction

While Paradox Junction is fun and easy to jump into, it feels like a wasted map slot as it's quite literally just two versions of Nuketown, and a randomly thrown-in Blundergat that seems disrespectful to MOTD and BOTD.

S-Tier Standard Maps

These maps are S-Tier for me, and if I'm going to replay any map, it's going to be one of these due to their map layout, wonder weapons, and strong impressions.

Totenreich

  • Totenreich Easter Egg Guide

Richtofen is my favorite COD Zombies character, so to see him get a personalized map with Totenreich already gave it bonus points in my book. However, I didn't expect the return of the Origins robots and a literal ice giant boss fight that dialed Totenreich from a potential 10/10 to an 11/10.

I love the map layout of Totenreich, from the connecting Fishery Island and Beacon Island, to the abandoned villages scattered throughout. Even the wonder weapon quest is a blast, and the Jotunn Star (while underwhelming over Round 40) is so much fun to hack-and-slash.

Ashes of the Damned

  • Ashes of the Damned Easter Egg Guide

The launch map of Black Ops 7 Zombies was, and still is, a delight. I may be biased because I love the return of the OG four (Richtofen, Dempsey, Takeo, and Nikolai), but that doesn't take away from the fact that Ashes of the Damned is a Tranzit-inspired map done right.

This map is huge, and driving Ol' Tessie around to visit different-but-familiar areas, which are equally fun to train zombies in, is a blast. I love the Necrofluid Gauntlet with Melee Macchiato, and I love taking Ol' Tessie for a spin to different areas if I ever get bored of being in one spot.

B-Tier Standard Maps

B-Tier maps are the ones I'd see as "decent, but with issues". I wouldn't begrudge playing these maps, but there are some things that could be improved to make them a far better experience.

Astra Malorum

  • Astra Malorum Easter Egg Guide

The intro cutscene, landing on a gigantic skull orbiting Saturn, and going to Mars... what a trip! Astra Malorum excels in letting the mayhem of the Dark Aether loose, until you actually explore the map and realise it's pretty small, and there isn't all that much to do other than shoot zombies.

The map can be pretty fun, and unlocking Mars is an easy treat. However, the LGM-1 feels extremely weak to use, even with buffs, and O.S.C.A.R.? This Elite Enemy spawns... constantly... and he makes me dread running Astra Malorum simply because I don't want to have to sprint away every time he finds me.

C-Tier Standard Maps

C-Tier maps are those that I view as simply "okay", but I find to be completely lacking in anything stimulating. These are the maps I'd pick if I wanted to switch my brain off and shoot zombies.

Paradox Junction

  • Paradox Junction Easter Egg Guide

Paradox Junction was... disappointing, to say the least. Waiting for a new map, only to be given Nuketown (for the third time) was a shame. At least in Black Ops 4 , Nuketown (Alpha Omega) had more areas to explore, whereas Pardox Junction is quite literally just Nuketown post-nuke and pre-nuke.

Subscribe for deeper Black Ops 7 Zombies map insights

Subscribe to the newsletter for deeper Black Ops 7 Zombies coverage, including detailed map tier breakdowns, survival tactics, and wonder-weapon guides to help you compare maps and refine strategies, plus broader gaming coverage.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

The easter egg is actually decent, and if you want to shut your brain off and shoot zombies for camos and XP, then Paradox Junction is fine, but it's just in a weird spot. Why Nuketown? Why bring back the Blundergat? It's frustrating, because if this map was supposed to be a "prison" that the Warden set up, then why not just take us to Alcatraz?

All Survival Maps Tier List

Armor Passive

Why?

S Tier

Vandorn Farm

It's a classic spot from Black Ops 2, but better than ever. The training area is large and allows you to switch your brain off and just enjoy going through the rounds with no restrictions.

A Tier

Exit 115

Despite the narrowness of the road, there's more space than you'd think to train zombies and get to high rounds. Exit 115 is easy for fun and fast high round strats with little in the way to burden you.

B Tier

Ashwood

Ashwood does something interesting where you're restricted to the upper floors, as Pack-a-Punch is found in the mist below, filled with super sprinters. Regardless, training in Ashwood is extremely challenging due to its tight areas, especially with a Zursa in the area.

Zarya Cosmodrome

Another area from Ashes of the Damned made into a Survival Map, Zarya Cosmodrome is pretty restrictive, where you only really have a small box and a narrow ramp to train in, but there is fun to be had in the intensity.

C Tier

Mars

While visually cool, the anti-gravity mechanic and Uber Klaus make Mars a true burden to play. Anti-gravity slows zombies down and occurs too frequently (and should be manually activated).

Take a look at our Black Ops 7 Zombies Guide HUB, which covers all easter eggs, relics, wonder weapons, and more!

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7

Follow
Followed
Action
FPS
Sci-Fi
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews
Top Critic Avg: 65 /100 Critics Rec: 35%
Released
November 14, 2025
ESRB
Mature 17+ / Blood and Gore, Intense Violence, Strong Language, Suggestive Themes, Use of Drugs
Developer(s)
Treyarch, Raven Software
Publisher(s)
Activision
5 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL
PHYSICAL

Genre(s)
Action, FPS, Sci-Fi
Platform(s)
PC, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X, Xbox Series S
Powered by
item.content:encoded:
Black Ops 7 Zombies currently features 4 Standard maps and 5 Survival maps, and you might be wondering where each of these maps ranks on a tier list. Below, we've ranked all 4 Standard maps against each other, and all 5 Survival maps to see which is the most fun, replayable, and exciting experience.

For Standard maps, we've factored in wonder weapons, easter eggs, general layout, and most importantly: fun. Take a look at the tier list below and check to see where your favorite map landed on our tier list and compare it to your own!

All Standard Maps Tier List

Map

Why?

S-Tier

Totenreich

The general vibe of Totenreich is immaculate, and while the easter egg quest is a tad shorter than most, the boss fight, map layout, and wonder weapon are heaps of fun. Besides, who doesn't like more Richtofen lore?

Ashes of the Damned

The launch map has so much to offer and proves that Treyarch can still innovate. Driving around Ol' Tessie across multiple locations, each with their own aesthetics, is a treat that makes this large map feel so very alive.

A-Tier

None

We don't think there are any A-Tier maps in BO7 Zombies yet, but the next two planned maps could change that.

B-Tier

Astra Malorum

There's no skybox quite like Astra Malorum, and there's certainly a lot to enjoy in terms of side easter eggs. However, the closed-in areas make this map a tad challenging, and O.S.C.A.R. can be even more annoying than George Romero from COTD.

C-Tier

Paradox Junction

While Paradox Junction is fun and easy to jump into, it feels like a wasted map slot as it's quite literally just two versions of Nuketown, and a randomly thrown-in Blundergat that seems disrespectful to MOTD and BOTD.

S-Tier Standard Maps

These maps are S-Tier for me, and if I'm going to replay any map, it's going to be one of these due to their map layout, wonder weapons, and strong impressions.

Totenreich

  • Totenreich Easter Egg Guide

Richtofen is my favorite COD Zombies character, so to see him get a personalized map with Totenreich already gave it bonus points in my book. However, I didn't expect the return of the Origins robots and a literal ice giant boss fight that dialed Totenreich from a potential 10/10 to an 11/10.

I love the map layout of Totenreich, from the connecting Fishery Island and Beacon Island, to the abandoned villages scattered throughout. Even the wonder weapon quest is a blast, and the Jotunn Star (while underwhelming over Round 40) is so much fun to hack-and-slash.

Ashes of the Damned

  • Ashes of the Damned Easter Egg Guide

The launch map of Black Ops 7 Zombies was, and still is, a delight. I may be biased because I love the return of the OG four (Richtofen, Dempsey, Takeo, and Nikolai), but that doesn't take away from the fact that Ashes of the Damned is a Tranzit-inspired map done right.

This map is huge, and driving Ol' Tessie around to visit different-but-familiar areas, which are equally fun to train zombies in, is a blast. I love the Necrofluid Gauntlet with Melee Macchiato, and I love taking Ol' Tessie for a spin to different areas if I ever get bored of being in one spot.

B-Tier Standard Maps

B-Tier maps are the ones I'd see as "decent, but with issues". I wouldn't begrudge playing these maps, but there are some things that could be improved to make them a far better experience.

Astra Malorum

  • Astra Malorum Easter Egg Guide

The intro cutscene, landing on a gigantic skull orbiting Saturn, and going to Mars... what a trip! Astra Malorum excels in letting the mayhem of the Dark Aether loose, until you actually explore the map and realise it's pretty small, and there isn't all that much to do other than shoot zombies.

The map can be pretty fun, and unlocking Mars is an easy treat. However, the LGM-1 feels extremely weak to use, even with buffs, and O.S.C.A.R.? This Elite Enemy spawns... constantly... and he makes me dread running Astra Malorum simply because I don't want to have to sprint away every time he finds me.

C-Tier Standard Maps

C-Tier maps are those that I view as simply "okay", but I find to be completely lacking in anything stimulating. These are the maps I'd pick if I wanted to switch my brain off and shoot zombies.

Paradox Junction

  • Paradox Junction Easter Egg Guide

Paradox Junction was... disappointing, to say the least. Waiting for a new map, only to be given Nuketown (for the third time) was a shame. At least in Black Ops 4 , Nuketown (Alpha Omega) had more areas to explore, whereas Pardox Junction is quite literally just Nuketown post-nuke and pre-nuke.

Subscribe for deeper Black Ops 7 Zombies map insights

Subscribe to the newsletter for deeper Black Ops 7 Zombies coverage, including detailed map tier breakdowns, survival tactics, and wonder-weapon guides to help you compare maps and refine strategies, plus broader gaming coverage.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

The easter egg is actually decent, and if you want to shut your brain off and shoot zombies for camos and XP, then Paradox Junction is fine, but it's just in a weird spot. Why Nuketown? Why bring back the Blundergat? It's frustrating, because if this map was supposed to be a "prison" that the Warden set up, then why not just take us to Alcatraz?

All Survival Maps Tier List

Armor Passive

Why?

S Tier

Vandorn Farm

It's a classic spot from Black Ops 2, but better than ever. The training area is large and allows you to switch your brain off and just enjoy going through the rounds with no restrictions.

A Tier

Exit 115

Despite the narrowness of the road, there's more space than you'd think to train zombies and get to high rounds. Exit 115 is easy for fun and fast high round strats with little in the way to burden you.

B Tier

Ashwood

Ashwood does something interesting where you're restricted to the upper floors, as Pack-a-Punch is found in the mist below, filled with super sprinters. Regardless, training in Ashwood is extremely challenging due to its tight areas, especially with a Zursa in the area.

Zarya Cosmodrome

Another area from Ashes of the Damned made into a Survival Map, Zarya Cosmodrome is pretty restrictive, where you only really have a small box and a narrow ramp to train in, but there is fun to be had in the intensity.

C Tier

Mars

While visually cool, the anti-gravity mechanic and Uber Klaus make Mars a true burden to play. Anti-gravity slows zombies down and occurs too frequently (and should be manually activated).

Take a look at our Black Ops 7 Zombies Guide HUB, which covers all easter eggs, relics, wonder weapons, and more!

item.title: Anime Limitless Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
Anime Limitless is a super exciting Roblox game where you jump into a fast-paced anime world full of intense fights and awesome powers. The action never stops, and it feels just like stepping into your favorite anime adventures!

Codes make everything way more fun because they hand out free rewards that help you level up quicker. No more waiting forever to boost your stats or try new traits—working codes give you instant help so you can focus on the cool battles and epic wins. Here are the latest Anime Limitless codes you can grab right now!

All Anime Limitless Codes

Working Anime Limitless Codes

Code

Reward(s)

RELEASE

20 minutes of x2 All Stat EXP

BIGBOOST1K

5 Race and 5 Trait Spins

THANKSFORPLAYING

3 Race and Trait Spins

Expired Anime Limitless Codes

As soon as any code stops working, we will move it here.

These working codes are a total game-changer for anyone just starting out or trying to climb the ranks fast. Getting extra Stat EXP means your character powers up quicker during training sessions, so you unlock stronger moves and abilities without spending hours grinding alone.

Race and Trait Spins are even better—they let you roll for rare combinations that match your playstyle, like super speed or powerful attacks inspired by anime heroes.

How to Redeem Anime Limitless Codes

  • Open Anime Limitless on Roblox .
  • Tap the menu icon in the bottom left corner of your screen.
  • Select the Codes tab.
  • Carefully type or paste one of the working codes into the box.
  • Hit the Redeem button and watch your rewards roll in!

How to Get More Anime Limitless Codes

The developers share new promo codes pretty often, and the easiest way to catch them is by joining the official Discord server. That’s where the devs drop announcements, sneak peeks, and fresh codes straight from the team—plus you can chat with other players about tips and strategies.

We keep this article fresh with every new batch of working codes for this Roblox game, so the smartest move is to bookmark it and swing by every couple of days. That way, you’ll always know the latest ones without hunting around. Just remember to redeem them quickly before they expire, and you’ll be dominating those anime battles in no time!

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by

Codes make everything way more fun because they hand out free rewards that help you level up quicker. These working codes are a total game-changer for anyone just starting out or trying to climb the ranks fast. Getting extra Stat EXP means your character powers up quicker during training sessions, so you unlock stronger moves and abilities without spending hours grinding alone.

Race and Trait Spins are even better—they let you roll for rare combinations that match your playstyle, like super speed or powerful attacks inspired by anime heroes.

The developers share new promo codes pretty often, and the easiest way to catch them is by joining the official Discord server. That’s where the devs drop announcements, sneak peeks, and fresh codes straight from the team—plus you can chat with other players about tips and strategies.

We keep this article fresh with every new batch of working codes for this Roblox game, so the smartest move is to bookmark it and swing by every couple of days. That way, you’ll always know the latest ones without hunting around. Just remember to redeem them quickly before they expire, and you’ll be dominating those anime battles in no time!

item.title: Cook and Sell Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
What makes playing Cook and Sell on Roblox even better? Those special working codes! They drop free cash straight into your game so you can buy tons of ingredients right away, cook faster, and build up your shop without waiting forever.

With our full list of active Cook and Sell codes, you won't have to suffer from slow starts! No more slow starts—grab the working codes for this Roblox game below and level up your bakery today.

All Cook and Sell Codes

Working Cook and Sell Codes

Code

Reward(s)

YUM

150 Cash

Expired Cook and Sell Codes

There are currently no expired Cook and Sell codes, but keep an eye on this page for updates!

That extra cash helps you get going super fast. Imagine starting with more money—you can buy loads of ingredients from your desk and cook bigger batches of treats to sell. It makes your shop fill up quicker, customers come running in, and you finish goals to unlock new recipes way sooner.

Free cash from codes turns your little bakery into a busy, successful one in no time. You get to experiment with cooking without worrying about running out of cash, and it keeps this Roblox game exciting and fun every time you play.

How to Redeem Cook and Sell Codes

  • Open Cook and Sell .
  • Look for the codes button inside the game.
  • Carefully type or paste the code exactly as it shows and hit enter or the claim button.
  • If it works, you’ll see the Cash added to your balance right away!

How to Get More Cook and Sell Codes

Want even more free cash and surprises? You can join the official Alpaca Games Roblox group. There’s an official Alpaca Games Discord server too, where the devs sometimes share new codes and news—hop in and say hi to the community!

We check for fresh promo codes all the time and update this article the moment new ones drop, so the easiest thing is to bookmark this page and pop back every few days. That way, you never miss out on free goodies that help your bakery grow even faster.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

ESRB
Teen / Diverse Content: Discretion Advised, In-Game Purchases (Includes Random Items), Users Interact
Genre(s)
Game Creation System
Platform(s)
PlayStation 4, Xbox One, PC, Android, iOS, Meta Quest 2, Meta Quest Pro
Powered by

Free cash from codes turns your little bakery into a busy, successful one in no time. You get to experiment with cooking without worrying about running out of cash, and it keeps this Roblox game exciting and fun every time you play.
